# Customer Lifetime Value (LTV)

**Lead/Lag.** Leading indicator; changes in LTV inputs (ARPU, margin, churn) predict
future revenue value before it realises. [Verified: ADR-004 §5]

**Interpretation.** Estimated net value of profit generated by an average customer over
their full relationship with the business. Assesses whether acquisition and retention
investment is justified relative to [[kpis/cac|CAC]]. [Verified: ADR-004 §5]

**Formula.** ARPU × Gross Margin / Churn Rate (simplified model; cohort-discounted
models provide more precision for longer-duration cohorts).

**Data needs.** Average revenue per user (ARPU), gross margin rate, and churn rate;
cohort data required for more robust cohort-based estimation.
See also [[kpis/customer-churn]] for churn rate definition. [Verified: ADR-004 §5]

**Industry differences.** Subscription businesses have explicit recurring revenue, making
LTV tractable from public metrics; transactional businesses require cohort-based purchase-
frequency models to estimate equivalent lifetime value. [Verified: ADR-004 §5]

**Relevant domains.**
- [[domains/customer-strategy]]

- [[domains/revenue-growth]]

**Related frameworks.**
- [[frameworks/cac-ltv-analysis]] — compares LTV against CAC to assess unit economics
- [[frameworks/segmentation-clv]] — computes LTV by customer segment to identify high-value tiers
- [[frameworks/retention-economics]] — quantifies the LTV impact of improving retention
- [[frameworks/cohort-retention-analysis]] — tracks LTV evolution across customer cohorts
